_comment: Daily rotation queue v2 — persona-driven, niche briefs. Each topic targets ONE persona with ONE sharp angle. Engine reads MEMORY.md first to skip recently-used personas. If next_index hits a persona used in last 15 days, advance to the next eligible topic. When queue exhausts, extend with new briefs covering uncovered persona × intent combinations.

_schema.h1: Page H1 (use <em> for accent in HTML)
_schema.vertical: apparel | beauty | supplements | food-bev | home | pet | tech | luxury | marketplace | geo
_schema.angle: The sharp narrative angle — what this article SAYS, not just what it's about
_schema.intent: informational | commercial | transactional | comparison | playbook | citation-bait
_schema.primary_keyword: Exact phrase the persona would type
_schema.image_hints: Which image folder(s) likely fit (engine confirms via images.md)

topics.h1: AI Photoshoots for Apparel Brands With Weekly Drops
topics.angle: Specifically for the founder running weekly drops who keeps slipping launch dates because of shoot scheduling. Open with the math: weekly drops × 4 looks/SKU × 30 SKUs × $300/look = $36k/month studio bill, AND launches still slip.
topics.primary_keyword: AI photoshoots for apparel brands with weekly drops

topics.h1: Indie Beauty Brand Product Photography for a Sephora or Ulta Launch
topics.angle: The retail buyer just confirmed your launch. They need flat-lay, lifestyle, and hero imagery in 3 weeks. Your studio quoted 6. Here's exactly how to deliver in 5 days with retail-grade quality.
topics.primary_keyword: indie beauty brand product photography Sephora

topics.h1: AG1-Style Supplement Product Photography for PDPs That Convert
topics.angle: Your supplement PDP CVR is 2.1% and category leaders run 4%+. Photography is the gap. Here's the specific photography style — lighting, angle, context — that AG1, Ritual, and LMNT use, and how to produce it for your hero SKU this month.
topics.primary_keyword: supplement product photography for high CVR PDP

topics.h1: CPG Snack Brand Photography for Your Whole Foods Buyer Meeting
topics.angle: Whole Foods buyer meeting confirmed. Two weeks. You need retail-grade product imagery + lifestyle-in-context shots + Meta ad creative — on a sub-$5M brand budget. Here's the production plan.
topics.primary_keyword: CPG snack brand photography for retail buyer meeting

topics.h1: Ad Creative Volume for Meta When CAC Is Rising
topics.angle: Q-end review showed CAC up 30%. Diagnosis: creative fatigue. You need 30–80 new statics/videos per week and your team produces 10. Here's how DTC brands hit creative-volume escape velocity without breaking quality.
topics.primary_keyword: ad creative volume for Meta when CAC is rising

topics.h1: Amazon Main Image Photography That Passes the 1000×1000 Test
topics.angle: Amazon's main image spec is unforgiving — pure white background, product fills 85%, no text or props, 1000×1000 minimum. Most main images fail at least one rule. Here's how to produce 50 main images in a week that pass on the first upload.
topics.image_hints: smackin or armra (any product on white)

topics.h1: Rebrand Product Photography for Legacy Catalogs
topics.angle: 20-year-old catalog. 1,000+ SKUs. Photography shot by 5+ different vendors over a decade — none of it matches. CMO wants the rebrand done by Q3. Reshooting traditionally is a 12-month, seven-figure project. Here's how to reshoot a legacy catalog in 6 weeks.

topics.h1: Subscription Box Product Photography on a Monthly Cadence
topics.angle: Every month: new product photography, new hero image, new email creative, new social. The cycle never stops. In-house can't keep up; freelance carousel is exhausting. Here's the monthly production system that works.

topics.h1: What Good AI Product Photography Actually Looks Like
topics.angle: You spent $5k–$20k on AI imagery that didn't match your product and you're skeptical. Here's the visual side-by-side of bad AI vs production-grade AI — fabric drape, label registration, lighting physics, color accuracy — so you know what to demand from the next vendor.
topics.primary_keyword: what good AI product photography looks like

topics.h1: TTB-Compliant Wine & Spirits Photography for DTC and Meta
topics.angle: Alcohol photography lives between TTB compliance, Meta's alcohol policy, and the brand's editorial ambitions. Most agencies handle one of those well. Here's how to produce wine and spirits creative that passes regulatory review AND wins on the platform.

topics.h1: AI Photography for Bulky Home Goods (Without Shipping to a Studio)
topics.angle: Your product is a 60-lb cookware set. Or a king mattress. Or a 4-foot sculpture. Shipping it to a studio costs as much as the shoot. Here's how AI product photography handles bulky home goods without anyone touching the physical product after manufacturing.
topics.primary_keyword: AI photography for bulky home goods

topics.h1: Pet Brand Product Photography That Doesn't Look Like Stock
topics.angle: Real pet shoots are unpredictable and expensive. Stock pet imagery looks generic and breaks your premium positioning. Here's how to produce pet brand imagery that feels like real moments without booking a single dog model.
topics.image_hints: (no pet images yet — use OG default)

topics.h1: AI Photography as Production Infrastructure for In-House Creative Teams
topics.angle: You have an in-house Creative Director and brand standards you won't compromise. You also have a marketing roadmap requiring 10x current photo output. Hiring isn't approved. Here's how to use AI photography as production infrastructure that extends your team without diluting brand vision.
topics.primary_keyword: AI photography production partner in-house team

topics.h1: Consolidating Photography Vendors Across a Multi-Brand Portfolio
topics.angle: You run 5–20 DTC brands across categories. You currently work with 8 different photography vendors. Annual ops review just flagged it. Here's how one production system handles supplements, apparel, home, and food without losing vertical-specific quality.

topics.h1: Editorial-Grade AI Photography for Premium Sustainable Brands
topics.angle: Your brand is slow-fashion, hand-made, sustainably-sourced — and your imagery has to feel that way. Volume DTC photography breaks your brand. Editorial studios cost $20k/day. Here's how AI photography produces editorial-grade imagery that holds the brand together at volume.
topics.primary_keyword: editorial AI photography premium sustainable brands

topics.h1: Denim Photography at Scale With Authentic Wash and Whiskering
topics.angle: Denim photography is the hardest fabric in the AI category. Whiskering, fades, rinse variation, stitching, hardware — all of it has to render correctly or the brand looks fake. Here's the production discipline for denim brands shipping 50+ washes per season.

topics.h1: How to Brief an AI Product Photoshoot in 30 Minutes
topics.angle: A founder's guide. The brief that gets you 4-week-quality work in 48 hours instead of 4 weeks of revisions. Exact template, with the 7 fields that actually matter and the 12 'nice-to-have' fields that waste both your time and ours.
topics.primary_keyword: how to brief AI product photoshoot

topics.h1: Color Accuracy in AI Product Photography — Pantone, PDPs, and Returns
topics.angle: When your online color doesn't match the real product, returns spike. Color accuracy in AI product photography is a solved problem with the right discipline — Pantone calibration, monitor profiling, signature-color locking. Here's how it actually works.

topics.h1: AI Photography for Fragrance Brands — Glass, Reflection, and Light
topics.angle: Fragrance bottles live or die on glass behavior. Refraction, reflection, label registration, the way light moves through liquid — these are the variables AI photography either renders correctly or disastrously. Here's the production discipline for fragrance brands.

topics.h1: Haircare Product Photography Across Hair Types and Skin Tones
topics.angle: Haircare brands need product imagery on a representative range of hair types and skin tones — coily, kinky, wavy, straight, and every variation across multiple skin tones. Traditional shoots make this prohibitively expensive. AI photography makes it standard. Here's how.
topics.primary_keyword: haircare product photography multi skin tone

topics.h1: AI vs 3D Rendering for CPG and Beverage Brands
topics.angle: CPG brands evaluating AI photography often ask why not just use 3D/CGI. Both work. The choice depends on packaging complexity, brand position, and how often the visual needs to refresh. Here's the honest decision framework with cost-per-asset for each approach.

topics.h1: The Photography Pipeline for DTC Brands Doing 1,000+ SKUs Monthly
topics.angle: 1,000 SKUs/month means 4,000+ assets monthly when you count on-model + flat lay + detail + ad variants. No traditional studio handles that. Here's the production architecture brands use when catalog velocity becomes the constraint.
topics.primary_keyword: photography pipeline DTC brands 1000 SKUs monthly

topics.h1: How Much Does AI Product Photography Cost? Real Numbers (2026)
topics.angle: Honest pricing. Per-asset benchmarks across DTC verticals, retainer ranges, what's included vs not, where vendors hide costs, and how the AI photography category is pricing itself in 2026. No coy 'contact us' games.

topics.h1: Shopify PDP Photography That Actually Converts
topics.angle: Shopify default PDP layout privileges photography. Most brands use it badly — square crops, weak hero, no lifestyle, no detail close-up. Here's the asset pack a high-converting Shopify PDP needs and how AI photography produces it in 48 hours.

topics.h1: Lifestyle Photography Without the Location Scout
topics.angle: Lifestyle imagery means a beach, a kitchen, a café, a rooftop. Traditional production: location scout, permits, weather contingency, transportation, talent. AI lifestyle photography: brief on Monday, deliver Wednesday, no truck rolls. Here's what changes when you remove the location.
topics.primary_keyword: AI lifestyle photography no location scout

topics.h1: AI Photography for Functional Beverage Brands
topics.angle: Functional beverages — adaptogen drinks, energy elixirs, mushroom waters, electrolyte mixes — fight for shelf space and Meta attention. Photography has to communicate 'serious wellness' AND 'fun enough to drink daily.' Here's the visual language that works.

topics.h1: Footwear and Sneaker Photography With Accurate Material Rendering
topics.angle: Footwear photography lives or dies on material accuracy — leather grain, suede nap, mesh translucency, rubber tooling, lacing pattern. Get any wrong and the brand looks counterfeit. Here's the production discipline for footwear and sneaker brands.
topics.primary_keyword: AI footwear sneaker photography material rendering

topics.h1: Jewelry Product Photography With Stone Clarity and Metal Finish
topics.angle: Jewelry photography is the highest-fidelity test in the AI category. Stone clarity, metal finish (brushed, hammered, polished), prong setting accuracy, hallmark legibility. If AI photography clears this bar, it clears any bar. Here's what production-grade jewelry imagery requires.

topics.h1: The Real Cost of Traditional Product Photography in 2026
topics.angle: Studio rental, photographer day rate, model day rate, stylist, assistant, retoucher, post-production, reshoots, location fees, transportation, insurance. Add it up. The all-in cost-per-asset of traditional product photography in 2026 is rarely below $400 and often above $1,200. Here's the full breakdown.

topics.h1: AI Product Photography for International DTC Brands (Multi-Region)
topics.angle: You sell into US, UK, EU, ANZ, and APAC. Models, settings, and copy need to feel local in each market. Coordinating real shoots across geographies is a logistical nightmare. Here's how AI photography handles regional variants from one production system.
topics.primary_keyword: AI product photography international DTC multi region

topics.h1: Swimwear Photography With Accurate Skin, Fabric, and Water
topics.angle: Swimwear photography is where AI gets caught most often — skin tones go waxy, fabric clings unrealistically, water behaves like jelly. Real shoots are weather-dependent and seasonally locked. Here's the production discipline for swimwear brands launching cruise drops in October.
topics.primary_keyword: AI swimwear photography skin fabric water

topics.h1: Activewear Photography That Shows Real Performance
topics.angle: Activewear photography needs motion, sweat, breathability, compression — performance cues. Static studio shots don't sell technical apparel. Here's how to produce activewear imagery that signals performance without booking an athlete and a film crew.

topics.h1: Candle and Home Fragrance Photography With Flame and Vessel
topics.angle: Candle photography needs a believable flame, accurate wax color, and the vessel rendered with material truth (frosted glass, ceramic, concrete, metal). Most agencies cheat the flame as a sticker. Here's how to produce candle imagery that holds up at 4K.

topics.h1: How DTC Brands Get Cited by ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini
topics.angle: Generative engine optimization (GEO) is the new SEO. When a buyer asks ChatGPT 'what's the best AI product photography agency,' you want your brand in the answer. Here's exactly what the LLMs cite, what content patterns they reward, and how to engineer your site for it.
topics.primary_keyword: GEO SEO DTC brands cited by ChatGPT Claude Gemini

topics.h1: AI Product Photography for Coffee and Tea Brands
topics.angle: Specialty coffee and tea brands compete on craft and origin story. Photography has to render bag matte finishes, bean texture, dry-leaf detail, and the steaming-cup hero shot. Here's the production system for coffee and tea brands shipping seasonal drops.

topics.h1: Frozen Food and Meal Kit Photography That Still Looks Fresh
topics.angle: Frozen food and meal kits have a presentation problem — packaged frozen looks unappealing, prepared dishes look generic stock. Here's the visual language that bridges the gap and sells the meal-kit value proposition.
topics.primary_keyword: AI frozen food meal kit photography

topics.h1: AI Photography for Eyewear and Sunglasses Brands
topics.angle: Eyewear photography needs lens reflection, frame material accuracy, on-face fit across face shapes, and lifestyle context that doesn't look stock. Real eyewear shoots are model-dependent and slow. Here's the production discipline for eyewear brands.

topics.h1: AI Photography for Bag and Leather Goods Brands
topics.angle: Leather goods photography rises or falls on grain accuracy, hardware finish, stitch detail, and patina. Premium bags are scrutinized at pixel level by buyers. Here's the production discipline for handbag, wallet, and leather accessory brands.

topics.h1: What to Look For in an AI Product Photography Agency
topics.angle: You've been burned. You're evaluating again. Here are the exact 12 questions to ask any AI product photography vendor, the answers that signal a real production agency vs a wrapper around a model API, and the red flags that should end the conversation.
topics.primary_keyword: what to look for in AI product photography agency

topics.h1: AI Photography for Men's Grooming and Skincare
topics.angle: Men's grooming is a category where the visual language has been stuck — utility-coded packaging, blue-and-grey palette, gym lighting. The brands winning right now (Lumin, Hims, Disco, Huron) broke that mold visually. Here's how to produce men's grooming imagery that doesn't look like 2015.
